title: DualSurfaceComparison
description: Set 070 S2 - the provenance-tagged merge of one dual-surface verification run (push + pull arms over the same committed state, provider and adversarial framing held equal). Written as dual-surface-comparison.json. Every finding is labeled by the surface(s) that caught it - push-only / pull-only / both. The load-bearing rule (from the Set 069 S6 floor-ratchet coverage lesson): a free-text description is NOT an identity - two findings merge to 'both' ONLY when they share a non-empty, operator/harness-assigned defectKey, never on wording. The runtime contract is the pure-Python validator ai_router.dual_surface_verify.validate_comparison_artifact, which also enforces cross-field invariants this schema cannot express: a 'both' finding's contributors must cover BOTH surfaces (and carry a defectKey), a 'push-only'/'pull-only' finding's contributors must be exactly that one surface, and provenanceComplete may be true only when no finding is unkeyed.

allOf.$comment: provenanceComplete=true requires both unkeyed counts to be 0 AND no finding may be unkeyed (defectKey == ''). Both halves are encoded here, matching validate_comparison_artifact exactly - a schema-only consumer rejects the same artifacts the runtime does.

sessionSetName.description: The set this comparison belongs to (identity).

comparedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the merge (the caller stamps it).

runTag.description: How the run was triggered. 'sampled' = unbiased random-sample telemetry; 'opt-in' = operator/orchestrator operational high-assurance. The two are NEVER pooled by the scorer.

committedRef.description: The diff range the push arm reviewed (provenance), e.g. 'base..head' or 'base..WORKTREE'.

attestation.description: The equal-arms attestation echoed from the dual-surface run (provider/model/framing held equal across arms). Free-form object - the runner derives it.

provenanceComplete.description: True only when EVERY finding on both arms carried a stable defectKey. When false the push-only/pull-only tallies are an UPPER BOUND (an unkeyed defect both arms caught appears as two single-arm entries). When true, pushUnkeyed and pullUnkeyed MUST both be 0 AND no finding may be unkeyed (defectKey == '') - the top-level if/then encodes BOTH halves, matching the runtime validator.

pushUnkeyed.description: Count of push-arm findings with no defectKey (un-mergeable; left as single-surface entries).

pullUnkeyed.description: Count of pull-arm findings with no defectKey (un-mergeable; left as single-surface entries).

findings.description: The merged, provenance-tagged findings. May be empty (both arms clean).

defectKey.description: The stable, operator/harness-assigned defect identity. Empty string for an unkeyed finding (always single-surface). On the Set 069 benchmark this is the ground-truth case id.

provenance.description: Which surface(s) caught this defect. 'both' requires a defectKey and contributors covering both surfaces (enforced by the runtime validator).

severity.description: The most-severe severity across contributing arms (verbatim, not normalized).

surfaces.description: The DISTINCT surface(s) that contributed (the de-duplicated summary of the contributors' surfaces). Full multiplicity is preserved in contributors.

contributors.description: Each arm's contribution, attribution preserved (a merge never discards either arm's wording).
